ques: I am not able to do Maths questions, what should I do?
ans: Start with basic questions, revise formulas daily, and practice step-by-step instead of jumping to hard problems.

ques: I forget formulas in the exam, how can I remember them?
ans: Make a small formula notebook, revise it every day, and try to understand how formulas are derived.

ques: Physics feels very tough to me, how can I improve?
ans: Focus on concepts first, then solve numericals daily and analyze your mistakes.

ques: Chemistry has too much to memorize, what should I do?
ans: For Inorganic, revise NCERT repeatedly. For Organic, understand reaction mechanisms. For Physical, practice numericals.

ques: I get very low marks in mock tests, should I stop preparing?
ans: No. Mock tests help you find weak areas. Work on your mistakes instead of getting demotivated.

ques: I cannot manage school and JEE preparation together, what can I do?
ans: Make a daily timetable and align JEE topics with your school syllabus to save time.

ques: I panic during exams even after studying, what should I do?
ans: Practice mock tests in exam-like conditions and use deep breathing to stay calm.

ques: I feel others are better than me, what should I do?
ans: Do not compare with others. Compare your present performance with your past performance.

ques: I cannot complete the syllabus on time, what should I do?
ans: Focus on high-weightage chapters first and revise them properly instead of rushing through everything.

ques: I make silly mistakes in exams, how can I reduce them?
ans: Read the question carefully, avoid rushing, and recheck calculations if time allows.

ques: I understand concepts but cannot solve questions, what should I do?
ans: Solve more solved examples and then practice similar problems to convert theory into application.

ques: I forget what I studied after a few days, what is the solution?
ans: Revise topics weekly and use short notes to quickly refresh important formulas and concepts.

ques: Maths takes too much time in exam, how can I improve speed?
ans: Practice timed questions daily and learn shortcuts and standard results for common question types.

ques: I am weak in coordinate geometry, what should I do?
ans: Start from straight lines and circles, practice basic problems, and gradually move to tougher ones.

ques: I make calculation mistakes in Physics and Maths, how can I avoid them?
ans: Write steps clearly, avoid mental calculation for long values, and recheck units and signs.

ques: Organic Chemistry reactions confuse me, what should I do?
ans: Focus on reaction mechanisms and practice converting one functional group into another.

ques: I feel sleepy while studying, what should I do?
ans: Study in short focused sessions, take small breaks, and avoid studying immediately after heavy meals.

ques: I cannot concentrate for long hours, how can I improve focus?
ans: Keep your phone away, study with a timer (like 25 minutes), and set small daily targets.

ques: I get demotivated after seeing tough questions, what should I do?
ans: Remember that tough questions are for practice, and improvement comes from regular effort.

ques: I do not know which chapters to prioritize for JEE, what should I do?
ans: Start with high-weightage and scoring chapters and revise them properly before moving to others.
